title: Data Flow
description: How data moves between the browser, Next.js server, and Supabase — client state, server state, auth, and storage.

nodes.label: React Components
nodes.tooltip: UI layer rendering pages and interactive elements. Consumes server state via Tanstack Query and client state via Zustand.

nodes.label: Tanstack Query
nodes.tooltip: Manages server data with caching, background refetch, and optimistic updates. Uses custom fetch wrapper from src/libs/fetch.ts.

nodes.label: Zustand Store
nodes.tooltip: Local client state with createSelectors pattern. Stores: edit-wizard, create-wizard, context-store. Persisted to localStorage where needed.

nodes.label: API Routes
nodes.tooltip: 60+ RESTful API routes handling all server-side operations. Uses Supabase server client with cookie-based auth.

nodes.label: proxy.ts
nodes.tooltip: Next.js 16 middleware handling session refresh, auth redirects, and onboarding checks on every request.

nodes.label: Auth
nodes.tooltip: Supabase Auth with cookie-based sessions via @supabase/ssr. Handles signup, login, password reset, and email change.

nodes.label: PostgreSQL
nodes.tooltip: Primary database with Row Level Security on all tables. Server client uses publishable key; admin client uses secret key for elevated operations.

nodes.label: Storage
nodes.tooltip: Two storage buckets: listing-images for product photos and thumbnails, profile-assets for member avatars and shop banners/hero images.
